ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Maximus, Bishop of Salona.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Although to what was faulty in thy ordination at the first thou hast added serious evil through the fault of disobedience, yet we, tempering with becoming moderation the authority of the Apostolic See, have never been incensed against thee to the extent that the case demanded.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But our displeasure which thou hadst excited against thyself continued the longer in that a sense of the responsibility entrusted to us tormented us exceedingly, lest we might seem to be passing over without attention certain unlawful doings of thine that we had heard of.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, if thou considerest well, thou wilt see that thou thyself, by deferring to satisfy us, didst confirm these reports, and thereby didst exasperate us the more against thee.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But now that, following wholesome counsel, thou hast submitted thyself humbly to the yoke of obedience, and that thy love, in doing penance, has purged itself, as we directed, by fitting satisfaction, understand thou that the favour of brotherly charity is restored to thee, and give thanks that thou art received into our fellowship: for, as it becomes us to be strict with those who persevere in a fault, so does it to be kind in pardoning those who return to a better mind.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now, therefore, that thy Fraternity knows that he has recovered the communion of the Apostolic See, let him send some one to us, according to custom, to receive and convey to him the pallium.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, whilst we do not suffer unlawful things to be perpetrated, we no less refuse not what is customary.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, though the discharge of the duties of our position might have called upon us to concede this, yet we are greatly constrained thereto by the request of our most sweet and excellent son, the lord Exarch Callinicus, that we would treat thee with moderation.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: His most dear wish we cannot resist, nor can we cause him sorrow.
